resources.string: Camera permission is required to scan barcodes.
Camera permission is required to scan nutrition labels.
Point at the nutrition label, then capture
Writes logged meals and water from Food.
Raise water consistency on tracked days first.
Anchor the week with protein, fiber, and calmer sodium.
Plan fruit, vegetables, and one fish meal into the week.
Keep this pattern and repeat the strongest logged days.
Log food to build a weekly score.
Track a few meals and water to see weekly patterns.
Search or scan a barcode to %1$s your first food.
No saved food matches “%1$s”. Scan a barcode or create it.
Foods you favorite show up here for one-tap %1$s.
Your unsaved recipe edits will be lost.
Add food to build this meal.
Create a meal or recipe instead
No recipes match this search yet.
No saved recipes yet — build one with New recipe.
Compare saved foods or Open Food Facts products per 100 g.
Turn a meal off to hide it from the diary. Anything already logged there still counts toward your day.
Some food data couldn\'t be refreshed.
You can plan up to 1 week ahead.
Failed to update Health Connect sync
Failed to sync Food to Health Connect
Voice placeholder ready. Review before logging.
Photo placeholder ready. Review before logging.
Enter fasting and eating hours that total 24
End date must be after start date
Keep at least one meal visible
Add at least one valid template item
Review and correct nutrition before saving.
Couldn\'t read the label — enter the values manually.
Product not found. Add details to create it.

plurals.name: food_message_synced_meals_and_water
plurals.item: Synced %1$d meal and water to Health Connect
Synced %1$d meals and water to Health Connect

resources.string: A little short of your protein target.
On pace for your protein target.
Fiber is in a strong range.
High Protein mode is on track.
High Protein mode needs more lean protein today.
Low-carb mode is within today\'s carb target.
Low-carb mode is over today\'s carb target.
Muscle Gain mode has enough energy and protein.
Muscle Gain mode needs more calories or protein.
Weight Loss mode is controlled without losing protein.
Weight Loss mode needs tighter calories or more protein.
Mediterranean-style mode emphasizes fiber, fish or legumes, and unsaturated fats.
Clean Eating mode emphasizes protein, fiber, lower sugar, and calmer sodium.
Rated against your custom calorie and nutrient targets.
Balanced mode checks calories, protein, fiber, and sodium together.
Protein is low for this meal.
Add eggs, yogurt, fish, meat, tofu, or legumes.
Add fruit, whole grains, or vegetables.
Sodium is high for one meal.
Balance it with lower-sodium choices later.
Calories are above the meal target.
Add protein or fiber if you are still hungry.
Protein, fiber, sodium, and calories look balanced.
Repeat this structure when it fits your day.
Sugar is high for one food.
Pair it with a lower-sugar choice next.
Sodium is high for one food.
Keep the next fat source lighter.
Calories are not backed by much protein.
Fiber is light for the carbs.
Add fruit, veg, beans, or whole grains.
This food fits today\'s pattern well.
Keep it when it supports your meal.
Add fruit as a snack or side.
Plan fish or seafood this week.
Marked locally for correction. Review serving size and nutrition before relying on it.
This saved food has little nutrition data. Correct it before frequent logging.
Label scans are best-effort. Check extracted values before saving or repeated logging.
Imported from barcode data. Serving size and brand should still be checked.
Created locally. Accuracy depends on the values entered in MusFit.
Start with a meal or favorite.
Log a meal, favorite, or quick calories to see today clearly.
You are over %1$s mg. Choose lower-sodium foods next.
Add about %1$s g protein to move toward goal.
Add %1$s g fiber with fruit, oats, beans, or veg.
Good protein and fiber for this meal.
Calories, protein, fiber, and sodium are aligned with your goals.
A lean protein serving would close the biggest gap.
A high-fiber side would improve today quickly.
Use protein plus carbs or veg to finish the day cleanly.
Today is balanced against your current goals.
Food does not have a weekly training signal connected yet.
No logged food days in this window.
Most logged days align with calorie, protein, fiber, and sodium targets.
Several logged days are close, with a few nutrition gaps.
Logged days often miss protein, fiber, sodium, or calorie targets.
Water is consistently close to goal on tracked days.
Water is partly covered, but there is room to tighten consistency.
Water is a clear weekly opportunity.
Fruit, vegetables, and fish all appeared this week.
Fruit, vegetables, and fish were not detected in logged food names.
High sodium is pulling today down.
Choose lower-sodium foods for the next meal.
Fiber is low for the day.
Add fruit, oats, beans, or vegetables.
Calories, protein, fiber, and sodium are aligned.
Keep the same pattern for the next meal.
2 scrambled eggs and a slice of toast…
Amounts below are approximate. Adjust anything before logging.
Per 100 g, imported values. Review and edit before saving — imports aren\'t always exact.
No recipes match this filter yet.
A practical calorie and macro split for general tracking.
Fruit or vegetables at two meals
Plan simple repeatable meals, then adjust portions by hunger and progress.
Raises protein while keeping carbs and fats moderate.
Build templates around eggs, yogurt, fish, meat, tofu, or legumes.
Adds calories and carbs while keeping protein high.
Plan larger lunches and dinners so calories do not pile up late.
Moderates calories while protecting protein and fiber.
Pre-plan snacks and dinner portions before the day gets busy.
Keeps carbs low and uses fats for more energy.
Plan protein plus low-carb vegetables before adding fats.
Emphasizes higher fiber carbs, unsaturated fats, and fish or legumes.
Plan bowls, salads, fish dinners, and legume-based lunches.
Focuses on protein, fiber, lower sugar, and less sodium.
Prep a protein, a grain or potato, and chopped vegetables for fast meals.
%1$.0f kcal · %2$.0f g protein · %3$.0f g carbs · %4$.0f g fat
A gentle overnight fast with an even eating window.
A moderate fast that still leaves room for three meals.
A focused daily fasting window with a compact eating window.
Set a custom fasting and eating split that totals 24 hours.
Health Connect needs install or update
This removes “%1$s” from the day. You can undo right after.
“%1$s” is removed from your database. Diary entries keep their logged values.
